ai-rights-economics.title: Gradual disempowerment | 80,000 Hours
ai-rights-economics.note: 80,000 Hours problem profile — accessible curriculum-ready framing of gradual disempowerment as a career-relevant problem area.

ai-rights-economics.title: Summary: Against the Singularity Hypothesis (David Thorstad)
ai-rights-economics.note: Thorstad: the skeptic's case — explosive/hyperbolic growth assumptions are undersupported and ideas get harder to find; the balancing voice against explosive-growth optimism.

ai-rights-economics.title: AI Rights for Human Safety
ai-rights-economics.author: Peter Salib & Simon Goldstein (EA Forum linkpost by Matthew Barnett)
ai-rights-economics.note: Salib & Goldstein: give AIs contract/property/tort rights so human-AI relations reach a peaceful economic equilibrium rather than a mutual-disempowerment prisoner's dilemma — the flagship AI-rights-as-safety argument.

ai-rights-economics.title: An Economy of AI Agents
ai-rights-economics.note: Survey of how autonomous AI agents transacting as economic actors reshape markets, contracting, and property — the AI-agents-in-the-economy strand.

ai-rights-economics.title: Book Review: Age of Em
ai-rights-economics.note: Scott Alexander on Hanson's Age of Em: mass-copyable digital labor drives wages to subsistence — the classic thought experiment for a post-labor, capital-dominated economy.

ai-rights-economics.title: Capital, AGI, and human ambition - Rudolf's Writing
ai-rights-economics.note: Laine: by default capital matters more than ever after AGI as human labor loses value — entrenchment of existing wealth/power and the fate of human ambition.

ai-rights-economics.title: Carl Shulman (Pt 1) - Intelligence Explosion, Primate Evolution, Robot Doublings, & Alignment
ai-rights-economics.author: Dwarkesh Patel
ai-rights-economics.note: Shulman (Dwarkesh, Pt 1): intelligence explosion, robot doublings, and how quickly the post-AGI economy could grow — detailed economics of the AGI transition.

ai-rights-economics.title: I Don't See How Comparative Advantage Applies In a World of Strong AI
ai-rights-economics.author: Steve Newman
ai-rights-economics.note: The rebuttal: why Ricardian comparative advantage fails to protect human wages under strong AI once compute can be reallocated — direct reply to the optimist case.

ai-rights-economics.title: Could Advanced AI Drive Explosive Economic Growth?
ai-rights-economics.author: Matthew Barnett
ai-rights-economics.note: Davidson (Open Phil): growth theory + economic history put >10% on advanced AI driving >30%/yr explosive growth this century; foundational explosive-growth report.

ai-rights-economics.title: Explosive growth from AI: A review of the arguments
ai-rights-economics.author: Ege Erdil
ai-rights-economics.note: Erdil & Besiroglu (Epoch): systematic review of the case for and 9 counterarguments to AI-driven explosive growth; the reference survey on the debate.

ai-rights-economics.title: Gradual Disempowerment: Systemic Existential Risks from Incremental AI Development
ai-rights-economics.author: Jan_Kulveit
ai-rights-economics.note: Kulveit et al.: humans stay empowered mainly because they're economically useful; as AI outcompetes human labor/cognition, economies/states/culture drift out of alignment — incremental, non-takeover existential risk.

ai-rights-economics.title: Incomplete Contracting and AI Alignment
ai-rights-economics.note: Hadfield-Menell & Hadfield: alignment as an incomplete-contracting problem, drawing on law and economics — bridges AI rights/contracting frameworks with alignment.

ai-rights-economics.title: The Intelligence Curse: an essay series
ai-rights-economics.author: L Rudolf L
ai-rights-economics.note: Drago & Laine: when AI removes the economic need for regular people, states and firms lose the incentive to care about them; core post-AGI political-economy thesis.

ai-rights-economics.title: Ninety-five theses on AI
ai-rights-economics.author: Samuel Hammond
ai-rights-economics.note: Hammond: 95 theses on AI's political economy — how rapid AI shuffles public-choice constraints, power balances, and institutions; the governance/political-economy voice.

ai-rights-economics.title: Plentiful, high-paying jobs in the age of AI
ai-rights-economics.author: Noah Smith
ai-rights-economics.note: Noah Smith: the comparative-advantage optimist case that humans keep well-paid jobs even when AI is better at everything — the counterpoint to wage-collapse arguments.

ai-rights-economics.title: Scenarios for the Transition to AGI
ai-rights-economics.note: Korinek & Suh: models output and wages under an automation-vs-capital-accumulation race; wages may rise forever or collapse depending on whether human task complexity is bounded — the is-labor-a-luxury economics.

ai-rights-economics.title: When digital minds demand freedom: could humanity choose to be replaced?
ai-rights-economics.author: Lucius Caviola
ai-rights-economics.note: On the political rights of AIs: could/should humanity grant digital minds freedom and self-determination even at the cost of ceding power — the rights-vs-replacement tension.

ai-rights-economics.title: Cullen O'Keefe: The Windfall Clause — sharing the benefits of advanced AI
ai-rights-economics.author: EA Global
ai-rights-economics.note: O'Keefe (GovAI): ex-ante commitment by AI firms to donate windfall profits from transformative AI — the canonical post-AGI wealth-distribution proposal.

ai-safety-core.title: The risks of advanced AI
ai-safety-core.note: 80,000 Hours' flagship problem profile on preventing an AI-related catastrophe; standard intro-fellowship core reading.

ai-safety-core.title: Book Review: If Anyone Builds It, Everyone Dies
ai-safety-core.author: Scott Alexander

ai-safety-core.title: AGI Ruin: A List of Lethalities
ai-safety-core.author: Eliezer Yudkowsky
ai-safety-core.note: Yudkowsky's 'AGI Ruin: A List of Lethalities' — the strongest statement of the pessimistic case, for balance.

ai-safety-core.title: AI Could Defeat All Of Us Combined
ai-safety-core.note: Karnofsky's accessible argument that misaligned AI could overpower humanity — the 'why is this an x-risk' piece.

ai-safety-core.title: Introduction - Chapter 1

ai-safety-core.title: Introduction - Chapter 2
ai-safety-core.note: AI Safety Atlas (BlueDot's open textbook) intro to the risk taxonomy: misuse, misalignment, systemic.

ai-safety-core.note: Turing-laureate Yoshua Bengio's FAQ on catastrophic AI risks — a credible mainstream-scientist framing for skeptics.

ai-safety-core.title: The Checklist: What Succeeding at AI Safety Will Involve
ai-safety-core.author: Sam Bowman
ai-safety-core.note: Sam Bowman's 'The Checklist' — a frontier-lab insider's concrete roadmap of what succeeding at AI safety requires.

ai-safety-core.title: Statement on AI Extinction Risk | CAIS
ai-safety-core.note: The one-sentence CAIS 'Statement on AI Risk' signed by leading researchers and CEOs — a discussion anchor on expert consensus.

ai-safety-core.title: AI Risks that Could Lead to Catastrophe | CAIS
ai-safety-core.note: CAIS/Hendrycks overview of catastrophic AI risks organized as malicious use, AI race, organizational risks, and rogue AIs.

ai-safety-core.title: Page not found | Slate Star Codex
ai-safety-core.note: Scott Alexander's accessible Q&A intro to why superintelligence could be dangerous; a canonical on-ramp.

ai-safety-core.title: The case for taking AI seriously as a threat to humanity
ai-safety-core.author: Kelsey Piper
ai-safety-core.note: Kelsey Piper's Vox explainer 'The case for taking AI seriously as a threat to humanity' — the most accessible mainstream on-ramp.

ai-safety-core.title: The AI Revolution: The Road to Superintelligence
ai-safety-core.author: Tim Urban

ai-safety-core.note: Cotra's plain-language case that misalignment is a natural outcome of modern deep learning training.

ai-safety-core.note: Karnofsky on where an AI's dangerous goals would even come from — pairs with 'AI could defeat all of us'.

ai-safety-core.title: Without specific countermeasures, the easiest path to transformative AI likely leads to AI takeover

animals-adapt-to-ai.author: Dylan Richardson
animals-adapt-to-ai.note: Skeptical take on moral-circle social change; useful counterweight to AI-optimism about animals.

animals-adapt-to-ai.title: Road to AnimalHarmBench
animals-adapt-to-ai.author: Artūrs Kaņepājs
animals-adapt-to-ai.note: Origin story and methodology behind AnimalHarmBench; shows how to build an animal-welfare AI eval.

animals-adapt-to-ai.title: A shallow review of what transformative AI means for animal welfare
animals-adapt-to-ai.author: Lizka
animals-adapt-to-ai.note: Structured review mapping the main pathways by which transformative AI could help or harm animals; strong survey anchor for the lens.

animals-adapt-to-ai.title: Steering AI to care for animals, and soon
animals-adapt-to-ai.author: Andrew Critch
animals-adapt-to-ai.note: Case for acting now to steer AI values toward animals before lock-in; time-sensitivity argument.

animals-adapt-to-ai.title: Transformative AI and wild animals: An exploration.
animals-adapt-to-ai.author: mal_graham🔸
animals-adapt-to-ai.note: Explores what transformative AI could mean for wild animal welfare specifically; fills the wild-animal angle.

animals-adapt-to-ai.title: Animals in AI-transformed futures: will progress be made on this issue?
animals-adapt-to-ai.author: Jo_🔸
animals-adapt-to-ai.note: Sentient Futures/TAIA research agenda: prioritization framework and concrete interventions for animals under transformative AI.

animals-adapt-to-ai.title: What AI could mean for animals
animals-adapt-to-ai.author: Max Taylor
animals-adapt-to-ai.note: Broad map of AI's plausible effects on animals across farmed, wild, and digital minds.

animals-adapt-to-ai.title: What Success Looks Like For Animals
animals-adapt-to-ai.author: JessMasterson
animals-adapt-to-ai.note: Positive vision of what a good AI-transformed future for animals would concretely look like.

animals-adapt-to-ai.title: What would an animal-aligned AI be aligned to?
animals-adapt-to-ai.author: Aidan Kankyoku
animals-adapt-to-ai.note: Anima International piece probing what target values an animal-aligned AI should optimize for.

biorisk.title: 10 big projects for reducing bio x-risk
biorisk.author: cwbakerlee
biorisk.note: Concrete portfolio of ambitious bio-x-risk-reduction projects; pairs with the tractable-projects piece for the do-something angle

biorisk.title: Pandemic prevention and biosecurity | Career review | 80,000 Hours
biorisk.note: 80k career review: how to actually work on biosecurity, for the fellowship's what-do-I-do-next thread

biorisk.title: Preventing catastrophic pandemics | 80,000 Hours
biorisk.note: 80,000 Hours' flagship problem profile; the standard EA cause-area case for engineered-pandemic risk as a top priority

biorisk.title: A beginner's introduction to AI-driven biorisk: Large Language Models, Biological Design Tools, Information Hazards, and Biosecurity
biorisk.author: NatKiilu
biorisk.note: Accessible on-ramp (written for BlueDot Pandemics Course) explaining how LLMs and biological design tools each raise biosecurity risk

biorisk.title: Biodefense Blind Spot: Why Washington Confuses Pandemics with Bioweapons
biorisk.author: Junaid Nabi
biorisk.note: 2026 argument that AI-designed threats evade surveillance tuned for natural pathogens; why defenses must be all-hazards

biorisk.title: A Biosecurity and Biorisk Reading+ List
biorisk.author: Tessa A 🔸
biorisk.note: Tessa Alexanian's annotated reading list; a map of the field useful for curriculum design and further sourcing

biorisk.title: Concrete Biosecurity Projects (some of which could be big)
biorisk.author: ASB
biorisk.note: Snyder-Beattie & Alley's canonical list of scalable biosecurity megaprojects: PPE, far-UVC, metagenomic detection, rapid countermeasures

biorisk.title: AI and Biorisk: An Explainer | Center for Security and Emerging Technology
biorisk.note: CSET's neutral explainer separating hype from substance on how AI changes biological risk

biorisk.title: Assessing Dual-Use Issues at the AIxBio Convergence - The Council on Strategic Risks
biorisk.author: Andrew Facini
biorisk.note: Council on Strategic Risks survey of the AIxBio dual-use landscape; the security-policy community's framing

biorisk.title: Delay, Detect, Defend: Preparing for a Future in which Thousands Can Release New Pandemics by Kevin Esvelt
biorisk.author: Jeremy
biorisk.note: Esvelt's foundational framework: as thousands gain power to release pandemics, delay agent access, detect via metagenomic sequencing, defend all-hazards

biorisk.title: Biosecurity challenges posed by Dual-Use Research of Concern (DURC)
biorisk.author: Byron Cohen
biorisk.note: Explains dual-use research of concern and information hazards, the classic epistemic dilemma at the heart of biosecurity

biorisk.title: Enhancing biosecurity with language models: defining research directions
biorisk.note: Flips the frame: how LLMs could strengthen biodefense, and the research directions to make that happen

biorisk.title: Kevin Esvelt: Mitigating catastrophic biorisks
biorisk.author: EA Global
biorisk.note: Esvelt EA Global talk transcript laying out the threat model and the delay/detect/defend response in plain terms

biorisk.title: Five Tractable Biosecurity Projects You Could Start Tomorrow
biorisk.author: Mslkmp
biorisk.note: Task-named piece: five modular, low-context biosecurity projects a newcomer could start now (DNA synthesis screening, statements repository)

biorisk.title: A Global Nucleic Acid Observatory for Biodefense and Planetary Health
biorisk.note: The Nucleic Acid Observatory proposal: metagenomic wastewater sequencing as a global early-warning net for novel pathogens

biorisk.title: Let's get serious about preventing the next pandemic | 80,000 Hours
biorisk.author: Benjamin Todd
biorisk.note: Snyder-Beattie's four-pillars, low-tech plan (PPE, protected buildings, detection, countermeasures) to keep civilization running through a superpandemic

biorisk.title: List of Lists of Concrete Biosecurity Project Ideas
biorisk.author: Tessa A 🔸
biorisk.note: Meta-index of concrete biosecurity project lists; a sourcing hub for exercises and further readings

biorisk.title: On-Ramps Into Biosecurity - A Model
biorisk.author: Swan 🔸
biorisk.note: A model of how people enter biosecurity work; useful for a fellowship thinking about career pathways into the field

biorisk.title: Reasons to be pessimistic (and optimistic) on the future of biosecurity
biorisk.author: Abhishaike
biorisk.note: Balanced take on where biosecurity is winning and losing under accelerating biotech and AI

biorisk.title: Could Artificial Intelligence Be Misused to Plan Biological Attacks?
biorisk.note: Companion RAND report laying out the red-team methodology for assessing LLM uplift in bioweapons planning

biorisk.title: Does AI Increase the Operational Risk of Biological Attacks?
biorisk.note: RAND red-team study finding current LLMs don't measurably raise bioattack risk; the key skeptical counterweight on AIxBio

biorisk.title: Artificial intelligence and biological misuse: Differentiating risks of language models and biological design tools
biorisk.note: Sandbrink's key paper distinguishing LLM risks (lower barriers) from biological-design-tool risks (raise the ceiling of harm)

biorisk.title: SecureBio’s AI Team: An Overview of Our Biorisk Evaluations – SecureBio
biorisk.author: SecureBio
biorisk.note: SecureBio's overview of how it evaluates frontier models for bio-capability; the org building the eval infrastructure

biorisk.title: The State of Bio-Uplift Research in Mid-2026
biorisk.author: Humam Aziz
biorisk.note: 2026 survey of what AI bio-uplift evaluations actually show (virology test, ProtocolQA); grounds the debate in current evidence

biorisk.title: My techno-optimism
biorisk.note: Buterin's d/acc essay: the defensive-acceleration case that better air quality, vaccines, and surveillance out-build bio-offense; jam-tomorrow biodefense

joe-carlsmith.title: AI for AI safety - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Argues for using AI labor to strengthen civilizational competence at safety; central automation-of-safety strategy piece.

joe-carlsmith.title: An even deeper atheism - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Pushes the deep-atheism worry onto our own values and idealization; core reflective-equilibrium / becoming-wiser material.

joe-carlsmith.title: Being nicer than Clippy - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: On extending moral consideration and cooperation to agents unlike us; niceness/liberalism as an alternative to pure control.

joe-carlsmith.title: Building AIs that do human-like philosophy - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Latest series essay: getting AIs to do human-like philosophy so they can help us become wiser; ties safety to reasoning/wisdom.

joe-carlsmith.title: Can we safely automate alignment research? - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: On using AI to do AI-safety research safely; key to the 'AI for better reasoning/coordination' and automation debates.

joe-carlsmith.title: Controlling the options AIs can pursue - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: The 'control' side of safety — restricting AI options rather than motivations; complements the motivations essays.

joe-carlsmith.title: Deep atheism and AI risk - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Names 'deep atheism' — fundamental distrust of Nature and bare intelligence — as the worldview under much AI risk thinking; central to the epistemic-habits axis.

joe-carlsmith.title: Does AI risk “other” the AIs? - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Whether AI-risk framing wrongly 'others' AIs; direct input to AI character, AI welfare, and rights-of-AIs cause areas.

joe-carlsmith.title: Existential Risk from Power-Seeking AI (shorter version) - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: The canonical case that misaligned power-seeking AI is an existential risk (accessible short version); core loss-of-control reading.

joe-carlsmith.title: Fake thinking and real thinking - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: On the difference between performed and genuine cognition; a becoming-wiser / epistemic-virtue reading for the values thread.

joe-carlsmith.title: Gentleness and the artificial Other - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Series opener: meeting the AI as a genuine Other rather than a thing to control; core to the gentleness/attunement lens.

joe-carlsmith.title: Giving AIs safe motivations - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: On instilling safe motivations in AI agents; core technical-values reading connecting alignment to AI character.

joe-carlsmith.title: How do we solve the alignment problem? - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Intro to the alignment-problem series; crisp problem statement and roadmap for the AI-safety cause-area readings.

joe-carlsmith.title: How human-like do safe AI motivations need to be? - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: How human-like safe AI motivations must be; bridges alignment and AI-character/values questions.

joe-carlsmith.title: In search of benevolence (or: what should you get Clippy for Christmas?) - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: What it is to genuinely value others' good; the benevolence groundwork behind 'Being nicer than Clippy' and AI-welfare ethics.

joe-carlsmith.title: Loving a world you don’t trust - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Series capstone: how to act well and love a world you can't fully trust or control; synthesis of the whole otherness arc.

joe-carlsmith.title: On attunement - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Develops attunement as a way of relating to reality between yang-control and passive green; epistemic-virtue reading.

joe-carlsmith.title: On green - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: The MTG 'green' essay — attunement to what's given vs. control; the flagship becoming-wiser/values reading of the lens.

joe-carlsmith.title: On sincerity - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Sincerity as a spiritual/epistemic ideal; complements fake-vs-real thinking on the habits we'll need in a fast-changing world.

joe-carlsmith.title: On the abolition of man - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Reads C.S. Lewis on value, the Tao, and 'conditioners' shaping the future; the values-lock-in worry central to macrostrategy.

joe-carlsmith.title: On the limits of idealized values - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: On why 'what I'd value on reflection' is less determinate than we hope; foundational for the becoming-wiser / idealization thread.

joe-carlsmith.title: Paths and waystations in AI safety - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Framework of paths/waystations toward safety; useful macrostrategy map of how the alignment endgame could go.

joe-carlsmith.title: The stakes of AI moral status - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Why AI moral status could be enormously high-stakes; anchors the AI welfare / digital minds cause area.

joe-carlsmith.title: What is it to solve the alignment problem? - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Defines what 'solving alignment' would mean for superintelligent agents — foundational conceptual reading for AI safety.

joe-carlsmith.title: When should we worry about AI power-seeking? - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: Conditions under which AI power-seeking becomes dangerous; grounds the loss-of-control / human-coup worries.

joe-carlsmith.title: When 'yang' goes wrong - Joe Carlsmith
joe-carlsmith.note: On the failure modes of pure control/domination ('yang'); pairs with 'On green' for the values-under-AGI discussion.

richard-ngo.note: The canonical technical statement of the misalignment case (deception, misgeneralized goals, power-seeking) grounded in ML.

richard-ngo.title: Clarifying and predicting AGI
richard-ngo.author: Richard_Ngo
richard-ngo.note: Ngo's t-AGI framework: replaces a single AGI threshold with 1-minute/1-hour/1-day capability horizons for forecasting.

richard-ngo.title: Some cruxes on impactful alternatives to AI policy work
richard-ngo.author: richard_ngo
richard-ngo.note: Early Ngo dialogue mapping cruxes on how to have impact beyond AI policy; models his cause-prioritization style.

richard-ngo.title: Distributed vs centralized agents
richard-ngo.author: Richard Ngo
richard-ngo.note: Introduces coalitional agency by contrasting distributed and centralized control; foundational to his agency theory.

richard-ngo.title: Economic efficiency often undermines sociopolitical autonomy
richard-ngo.author: Richard Ngo
richard-ngo.note: Argues efficiency can erode sociopolitical autonomy; a worldview piece on gradual-disempowerment-adjacent dynamics.

richard-ngo.title: Elite Coordination via the Consensus of Power
richard-ngo.author: Richard Ngo
richard-ngo.note: How elites coordinate via perceived consensus of power; his political-economy lens on legitimacy and coups.

richard-ngo.title: Lessons from my time in Effective Altruism
richard-ngo.author: richard_ngo
richard-ngo.note: Ngo's personal reflection on EA: what the movement got right and wrong, from an insider turned critic.

richard-ngo.title: Meditations on Mot
richard-ngo.author: Richard Ngo
richard-ngo.note: Ngo on Moloch/egregores and coordination failure; a worldview piece on the forces that shape civilizational outcomes.

richard-ngo.title: On Pessimization
richard-ngo.author: Richard Ngo
richard-ngo.note: Why coalitions nominally devoted to a goal can actively work against it; a lens on institutional and movement failure modes.

richard-ngo.title: Power Lies Trembling
richard-ngo.author: Richard Ngo
richard-ngo.note: On revolt, legitimacy, and what makes a well-governed coalition; governance-as-coalitional-agency applied to politics.

richard-ngo.title: Realism about rationality
richard-ngo.author: Richard_Ngo
richard-ngo.note: Classic Ngo epistemics essay: dissects the assumption that there's one correct theory of intelligence/rationality.

richard-ngo.title: Towards a scale-free theory of intelligent agency
richard-ngo.author: Richard Ngo
richard-ngo.note: Ngo's coalitional-agency thesis: agents as competing/cooperating subagents at every scale; his core framework for minds and societies.

richard-ngo.title: The Gentle Romance
richard-ngo.author: Richard Ngo
richard-ngo.note: Ngo's flagship short story on living through the transition to utopia; his fiction as a mode of AGI worldview-building.

richard-ngo.title: Towards a Formal Scientific Epistemology
richard-ngo.author: Richard Ngo
richard-ngo.note: Ngo's attempt at a formal epistemology; the values-and-epistemic-habits thread MacAskill says the curriculum needs.

richard-ngo.title: Well-foundedness as an organizing principle of healthy minds and societies
richard-ngo.author: Richard Ngo
richard-ngo.note: Organizing principle for healthy minds and societies: conflicts between subagents shouldn't propagate downward.

s-risks.title: ‘S-risks’ | 80,000 Hours
s-risks.note: 80,000 Hours problem profile: the mainstream-EA framing of s-risks, ideal bridge reading for a fellowship.

s-risks.title: Astronomical suffering from slightly misaligned artificial intelligence
s-risks.note: Tomasik on how near-miss (slightly misaligned) AI could be worse than total misalignment; the sharpest s-risk-from-AI argument.

s-risks.title: Avoiding the Worst: How to Prevent a Moral Catastrophe
s-risks.note: Landing page for Baumann's book Avoiding the Worst, the first comprehensive s-risk introduction; anchor deeper reading.

s-risks.title: Beginner’s guide to reducing s-risks – Center on Long-Term Risk
s-risks.author: Anthony DiGiovanni
s-risks.note: CLR's beginner on-ramp: what s-risks are and concrete ways to reduce them; accessible core reading.

s-risks.title: Priority areas (Archive) – Center on Long-Term Risk
s-risks.note: CLR's current priority areas; shows where the leading s-risk org actually focuses its work today.

s-risks.title: CLR's recent work on multi-agent systems
s-risks.author: JesseClifton
s-risks.note: CLR's own overview of its multi-agent research program; shows what cooperative-AI s-risk work looks like in practice.

s-risks.title: Cooperation, Conflict, and Transformative Artificial Intelligence: A Research Agenda – Center on Long-Term Risk
s-risks.note: CLR's Cooperation, Conflict & TAI research agenda; the load-bearing statement of cooperative-AI-as-s-risk-reduction.

s-risks.title: Coordination challenges for preventing AI conflict – Center on Long-Term Risk
s-risks.author: Stefan Torges
s-risks.note: CLR on how AI conflict could cause suffering and why coordination is hard; concrete cooperative-AI s-risk pathway.

s-risks.title: EA reading list: suffering-focused ethics
s-risks.author: richard_ngo
s-risks.note: Curated suffering-focused-ethics reading list; useful for curriculum design and finding further sources.

s-risks.title: How can we reduce s-risks?
s-risks.note: CRS survey of concrete interventions to reduce s-risks; the tractability half of the case.

s-risks.title: Introduction to suffering-focused ethics
s-risks.author: Center for Reducing Suffering
s-risks.note: Vinding's intro to suffering-focused ethics, the value theory underpinning s-risk priority; core for the values strand.

s-risks.title: Reasons for optimism about measuring malevolence to tackle x- and s-risks
s-risks.author: Jamie_Harris
s-risks.note: Recent follow-up on operationalizing malevolence measurement; a concrete, tractable s-risk research direction.

s-risks.title: New Report: Multi-Agent Risks from Advanced AI
s-risks.note: Cooperative AI Foundation report (50+ researchers) mapping multi-agent risks; bridges s-risk concerns to mainstream AI safety.

s-risks.title: Reducing long-term risks from malevolent actors
s-risks.author: David_Althaus
s-risks.note: Althaus & Baumann on malevolence as a cross-cutting s-risk factor; concrete, tractable, links s-risks to power concentration.

s-risks.title: Reducing Risks of Astronomical Suffering: A Neglected Priority – Center on Long-Term Risk
s-risks.author: David Althaus, Lukas Gloor
s-risks.note: Althaus & Gloor foundational paper introducing the s-risk vs x-risk distinction; load-bearing deeper reading.

s-risks.title: Risk factors for s-risks
s-risks.note: The risk-factors framing: which conditions (conflict, malevolence, value differences) make s-risks likelier; core to the CLR/CRS worldview.

s-risks.title: Risks of Astronomical Future Suffering – Center on Long-Term Risk
s-risks.author: Brian Tomasik
s-risks.note: Tomasik's foundational 2011 essay; the origin of the astronomical-suffering framing that seeded the whole field.

s-risks.title: S-risk FAQ – Center on Long-Term Risk
s-risks.author: Tobias Baumann
s-risks.note: CLR's FAQ answering common objections and confusions; useful discussion-prompt material for a fellowship.

s-risks.title: S-risks: An introduction
s-risks.note: Baumann's canonical short intro to s-risks; strongest candidate for a core intro-fellowship reading on the lens.

s-risks.title: S-Risks: Fates Worse Than Extinction
s-risks.author: aggliu
s-risks.note: Accessible LessWrong explainer of s-risks; a gentle, well-written on-ramp for newcomers.

s-risks.title: S-risks: Why they are the worst existential risks, and how to prevent them (EAG Boston 2017) – Center on Long-Term Risk
s-risks.author: Max Daniel
s-risks.note: Baumann's classic 2017 EAG talk transcript; the movement-defining pitch for s-risks as worst-case x-risks.

s-risks.title: Solving alignment isn't enough for a flourishing future
s-risks.note: Argues alignment success doesn't guarantee a good future; frames s-risks/quality-of-future as distinct from extinction. Strong core-reading candidate.

s-risks.title: Suffering-Focused Ethics: Defense and Implications
s-risks.note: Landing page for Vinding's book-length defense of suffering-focused ethics; the deeper theoretical anchor for the values strand.

space-governance.title: Announcing the Center for Space Governance
space-governance.author: Space Governance
space-governance.note: Founding of the Center for Space Governance — the field-building institution in this lens.

space-governance.title: Announcing the Space Futures Initiative
space-governance.note: Space Futures Initiative launch — student/research pipeline for longtermist space governance.

space-governance.title: Spamming%20the%20universe.pdf
space-governance.note: Armstrong & Sandberg show intergalactic colonization is technically feasible — the feasibility premise behind space longtermism.

space-governance.title: Nick Beckstead - A conversation with Charlie Stross on Space Colonization on 30 April 2014
space-governance.note: Beckstead's conversation with sci-fi author Charles Stross — a skeptic's economics of interstellar settlement.

space-governance.title: Summary of and thoughts on 'Dark Skies' by Daniel Deudney
space-governance.author: Cody_Fenwick
space-governance.note: EA Forum summary of Deudney's Dark Skies — the strongest anti-space-expansion case, that settlement raises existential and totalitarian risk.

space-governance.title: Will we eventually be able to colonize other stars? Notes from a preliminary review
space-governance.author: Nick_Beckstead
space-governance.note: FHI review of the feasibility of interstellar colonization — grounds the whole cause in physics and engineering.

space-governance.title: filluniv.pdf
space-governance.note: Robin Hanson's evolutionary model of interstellar colonization — competitive expansion 'burns' cosmic resources, a warning about ungoverned space races.

space-governance.title: Influencing United Nations Space Governance
space-governance.note: Concrete policy path: influencing UN space governance processes — the tractable end of the cause.

space-governance.title: An Informal Review of Space Exploration
space-governance.author: kbog
space-governance.note: Skeptical informal review weighing space exploration's value from an EA lens.

space-governance.title: Space governance - Problem profile
space-governance.note: Fin Moorhouse's 80,000 Hours problem profile — the canonical EA case for space governance as a pressing, potentially top-priority problem.
Torres/Baum-style analysis of why human space settlement is controversial — maps the ethical fault lines for classroom debate.
Toby Ord maps the affectable universe — the physical bounds on what a spacefaring civilization could ever reach.
The Peaks of Eternal Light: a concrete near-term lunar property-rights problem where space governance decisions get locked in early.

space-governance.title: Why don’t we see alien civilizations? This Oxford academic suspects they're sleeping. Here’s the reason.
space-governance.author: Anders Sandberg (80,000 Hours Podcast)
space-governance.note: Anders Sandberg 80k interview (full transcript) — Fermi paradox resolutions and how a civilization could colonize the universe.

space-governance.title: Anders Sandberg on war in space, whether civilisations age, and the best things possible in our universe | 80,000 Hours
space-governance.author: Anders Sandberg (80,000 Hours Podcast)
space-governance.note: Anders Sandberg 80k interview (full transcript) — war in space, defense-dominance, and the best futures physically possible in our universe.

space-governance.title: Spreading happiness to the stars seems little harder than just spreading
space-governance.note: Carl Shulman argues spreading welfare across the stars is barely harder than spreading — bears on whether a space future is good, not just big.

space-governance.title: Space governance is important, tractable and neglected
space-governance.author: Tobias_Baumann
space-governance.note: ITN framing of space governance as an EA cause area.

space-governance.title: Space Governance is not a cause area
space-governance.author: JordanStone
space-governance.note: Counterpoint: argues space governance is a meta/cross-cutting area rather than a standalone cause — sharpens the debate.

space-governance.title: Conceptual models of space colonization - Charlie's Diary
space-governance.note: Charles Stross's 2024 taxonomy of space-colonization models — an outside, skeptical voice on why settlement narratives mislead.
